dimension_notes.type_safety: Project is JavaScript (.js/.jsx), not TypeScript. No static typing anywhere in the runtime code. This is a principled gap — would need a migration to TS to address. Scores 72 because the pattern is consistent (JS throughout) rather than mixed.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: 25+ test files covering components, lib functions, API routes, and data integrity. Tests are parameterized and cover real domain logic (gap-scoring, report-generator). Mocks for external dependencies (leaflet, recharts) are properly set up. Scores well for JS project.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Clean separation: data layer (data/), pure functions (lib/), API routes (app/api/), React components. The gap-scoring algorithm is properly isolated as a pure function with documented weights. Dynamic import for WorldMap (leaflet SSR) shows architectural awareness.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: gap-scoring.js uses named constants for all magic numbers (FEASIBILITY_BASELINE, FEASIBILITY_FISH_BONUS, etc.) — good practice. Components make appropriate use of useMemo for expensive computations.
dimension_notes.advocacy_language_quality: Domain terminology is consistent — farmed aquatic animals, welfare gap, slaughterhouse used precisely. The AISuite component's terminology analysis correctly classifies welfare language strength. Species data uses scientific names and sourced production figures.
dimension_notes.advocacy_ux_inclusivity: The intervention simulator and report generator make findings accessible across detail levels. Progressive disclosure in SpeciesDetail. However, no internationalization, no offline mode, and no explicit low-bandwidth optimization.
dimension_notes.advocacy_data_sovereignty: All data is static/local (species.js, countries.js). No external data calls from the client beyond API routes. Clean data sovereignty posture for a research tool.
